commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer-labeled PR with proof and compatibility/availability decisions remaining; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the current merge diff; the stale dependency warning does not match the six-file TypeScript diff. (sha=5acbc3d03c291cfe25c8ee70b14b7053808c9411)
commands.review_summary:
This PR extracts Control UI protocol freshness detection into reusable helpers, registers a core Doctor health check for lint/dry-run reporting, excludes that check from the broad structured repair pass, and adds focused unit coverage.

PR surface: Source +87, Tests +134. Total +221 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new structured Doctor health reporting surface rather than fixing a reproduced bug. Source inspection verifies the intended code paths, but real CLI proof is still missing for the changed behavior.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled PR with proof and compatibility/availability decisions remaining; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: Doctor: expose UI freshness health findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes Control UI protocol freshness as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps the real build/rebuild mutation on the existing positional maybeRepairUiProtocolFreshness() path used by openclaw doctor --fix.
- Skips the UI freshness check in the broad structured repair bridge so this PR does not change existing repair ownership or prompt behavior.

This keeps the rule small: the existing UI repair flow now shares its detector with the health registry, and dry-run reports only a process effect when the source checkout can actually build the UI. It intentionally does not invent a file diff for generated UI assets.

## Verification

- pnpm install
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
- git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot - 5 files, 44 tests
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- bash /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main - clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openclaw doctor --lint can report missing/stale Control UI assets, and dry-run repair output can show the build/rebuild process effect without taking over the real UI repair.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0, OpenClaw source checkout based on current origin/main.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused doctor/UI Vitest files, focused oxfmt/oxlint, core tsgo, core test tsgo, and autoreview as listed above.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed with 5 files and 44 tests; both tsgo lanes exited 0; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: UI freshness is registered as core/doctor/ui-protocol-freshness; lint/dry-run can see structured findings/effects, while broad structured fix skips this id so the existing positional UI repair remains the only real mutating path.
What was not tested: a live UI rebuild was not run because this PR preserves the existing build command path and only adds structured reporting around its detector.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
